meta.disclaimer: Reasoned analytical estimates from live team-news research, not live bookmaker prices. Fun-money only — always check the actual odds before you stake.
meta.method: Per-match deep research on both squads (probable XIs, form, injuries, set-piece + penalty takers) via live web search, refreshed daily and again near kickoff when lineups are confirmed.

win.reason: Opta supercomputer gives Portugal 54.5% win vs 23.2% DR Congo, 22.3% draw. Portugal unbeaten in last 5 (3 straight friendly wins pre-tournament), arguably best midfield in the tournament (Vitinha, B.Fernandes, J.Neves, Bernardo Silva) plus deep attack. DR Congo set up to defend deep in a compact 4-5-1/5-3-2 and counter, which caps Portugal's margin but not the result.

scorers.note: Captain at his sixth WC, top scorer in Portugal's qualifying group (5 goals), penalty taker, scored in WC openers before (Ghana 2022). Central striker in Martinez's 4-2-3-1.
Sharp PSG finisher and the natural No.9 alternative/sub; reliable late-game scorer if Ronaldo is rotated or Portugal chase a second-half goal vs a tiring deep block.
Back in his favoured No.10 role, on penalties/free-kicks as backup, arrives in the box; record 21 PL assists this season but also a goal threat from range.
Direct wide threat cutting in on his stronger foot against a back five; one-on-one dribbler likely to find space late as DR Congo's block fatigues.

assists.note: Primary creator from the No.10 role; league's standout assist man (21 PL assists), main set-piece and through-ball supplier to Ronaldo.
Overlapping left-back delivering cutbacks/crosses against a compact block; one of Portugal's most advanced full-back outlets.
Deep-lying playmaker controlling tempo; line-breaking passes to release Neto/Bernardo and feed the box against a low block.

penalty.note: DR Congo defend deep with a packed box (Wan-Bissaka, Mbemba, Tuanzebe, Masuaku), raising the chance of a box foul over 90 minutes. Ronaldo is the established first-choice penalty taker, Bruno Fernandes the reliable backup.

lineups.home: Diogo Costa, Joao Cancelo, Tomas Araujo, Ruben Dias, Nuno Mendes, Joao Neves, Vitinha, Bernardo Silva, Bruno Fernandes, Joao Felix, Cristiano Ronaldo
lineups.away: Lionel Mpasi, Aaron Wan-Bissaka, Chancel Mbemba, Axel Tuanzebe, Arthur Masuaku, Meschack Elia, Samuel Moutoussamy, Noah Sadiki, Nathanael Mbuku, Cedric Bakambu, Yoane Wissa

playerNotes.note: 41, sixth World Cup, 226 caps / 143 goals (both records). First-choice penalty taker; led qualifying scoring with 5. The match's headline figure and main goal threat.
Back in his preferred No.10; record-breaking PL assist season. Primary creator, on free-kicks and backup penalties.
Defensive leader; had injury problems in the second half of the club season but is fit for the opener and expected to start at centre-back.
Star man and main attacking outlet; 9 international goals. Injury-hit, underwhelming Newcastle season, but DR Congo's chief counter-attacking threat.
Veteran striker, squad's top scorer (21 international goals); big-game experience, likely to feature as a focal point or impact option off the bench.
Premier League full-back anchoring a likely back five; key to containing Portugal's wide overloads from Neto and Nuno Mendes.

win.reason: England field their strongest XI with Kane in career-best form (61 goals for Bayern in 2025-26) and a deeper squad. Croatia leaving Kovacic on the bench weakens their midfield control against the Rice-Anderson pivot. England favored but Croatia's tournament experience and Modric keep it from being a rout.

scorers.note: England's penalty taker and the most prolific striker in the tournament (61 goals club 2025-26); central reference point and primary goal threat.
Driving from the No.10 role, arrives late in the box behind Kane; secondary goal threat from deep runs.
Starts on the left wing; pace in behind Croatia's back three, cutting inside onto his stronger foot.
Croatia's lone starting striker in red-hot form (12 in 13 MLS); plays his club football at this very AT&T Stadium for FC Dallas — main away threat fed by Baturina.

assists.note: Primary creator from the No.10, feeding Kane and the wingers.
Right-back and a corner/free-kick deliverer for England; overlaps Madueke and whips crosses for Kane.
Croatia's chief creative outlet in the advanced role; the designated supplier to Musa and a set-piece option.

penalty.backup: Bukayo Saka (England, from bench) / Ivan Perisic (Croatia)
penalty.note: Kane is England's automatic spot-kick taker. With Kovacic and Kramaric benched, Modric is Croatia's on-pitch penalty taker, with Perisic the next option and Budimir the bench backup. Modric, Perisic, Pasalic and Baturina share set-piece duties.

lineups.home: Jordan Pickford, Reece James, John Stones, Ezri Konsa, Nico O'Reilly, Elliot Anderson, Declan Rice, Noni Madueke, Jude Bellingham, Anthony Gordon, Harry Kane
lineups.away: Dominik Livakovic, Josip Sutalo, Luka Vuskovic, Josko Gvardiol, Josip Stanisic, Luka Sucic, Luka Modric, Ivan Perisic, Mario Pasalic, Martin Baturina, Petar Musa

playerNotes.note: Career-best form, 61 goals for Bayern in 2025-26 and second European Golden Shoe; penalty taker and focal point.
Starts at centre-back alongside Stones; Marc Guehi drops to the bench — a notable selection call.
Starts at left-back, the least-experienced England starter; Croatia may target his flank with Stanisic and Pasalic.
Captains at 40 after recovering from a cheekbone fracture; controls midfield and is the on-pitch penalty taker with Kovacic benched.
Preferred over Kramaric at striker; plays his MLS football at AT&T Stadium for FC Dallas, a familiarity edge in the venue.
BENCHED despite featuring in predicted XIs; his absence from the engine room weakens Croatia's ball progression against Rice and Anderson.

win.reason: Ghana carry the clear individual-quality edge in attack with in-form Semenyo (Premier League standout, market-leading ~30% anytime scorer probability) plus captain Jordan Ayew (7 goals in qualifying) and Nuamah running wide. Panama, despite Partey (visa) and Kudus (injury) being out, still face a Ghana front line that is more dangerous than the surprise Waterman-Barcenas pairing Christiansen has picked. Panama benching Fajardo, Diaz, Davis and Godoy weakens their central goal threat and set-piece pedigree, leaving them reliant on counters; Ghana should edge a tight, lower-scoring opener.

scorers.note: Unanimous market leader (~+230, 29.7% implied); virtually all Ghana's final-third output runs through him with Partey and Kudus absent.
Captain, penalty taker and qualifying talisman with 7 goals in 10 - Ghana's central focal point up top.
Panama's surprise starter and market-leading scorer threat (~+220/+300), 14 international goals, the counter-attack outlet up top.
Wide forward at ~4/1, direct runner who stretches Panama's deep block from the flank.

assists.note: Creates as much as he scores; Fox Sports flagged a Semenyo goal+assist double as a live angle.
Added 7 assists alongside 7 goals in qualifying - Ghana's primary creator-finisher link.
Panama's main transition outlet and a free-kick/corner taker now Davis and Carrasquilla are benched.

penalty.note: With listed pen-takers Davis and Carrasquilla both benched, Waterman is the on-pitch penalty option for Panama; for Ghana, Jordan Ayew remains the designated taker with a flawless senior record.

lineups.home: Ati-Zigi, Senaya, Adjetey, Opoku, Mensah, Owusu, Yirenkyi, Nuamah, Boakye, Semenyo, Ayew
lineups.away: Mosquera, Blackman, Cordoba, Ramos, Andrade, Murillo, Martinez, Harvey, Rodriguez, Barcenas, Waterman

playerNotes.note: In world-class form after a prolific Premier League season; the single most-cited player in the fixture and Ghana's whole attacking plan with Partey/Kudus out.
Captain, penalty taker and qualifying driving force (7G/7A in 10) at his third World Cup with 120 caps.
34-year-old surprise starter up front, 14 goals in 55 caps and Dimers' highest Panama scoring projection; now also Panama's on-pitch penalty option.
Captain in this XI (98 caps, 8 goals); a winger pushed forward, on free-kick and corner duty driving Panama's set pieces.
Key transition threat Christiansen trusts to create in the final third, plus free-kick and corner taker now Davis/Carrasquilla are benched.
Starts in central midfield with Godoy benched - a younger, more energetic legs option to screen and break up Ghana's link play.

win.reason: Markets price Colombia ~72% (-250/-267). Superior quality across the pitch vs Uzbekistan's World Cup debut, with the confirmed front of Suarez/Diaz/James/Arias the difference. Azteca altitude (~2240m) and a disciplined debutant low block temper the margin rather than the result.

scorers.note: Confirmed lone striker; Primeira Liga top scorer (38 in 53 for Sporting CP) on his tournament debut, the focal point of every Colombia attack.
Confirmed left wing; career-best form (15 G/14 A in a Bundesliga title season at Bayern); direct running targets the Uzbek right where Khusanov's match sharpness is questioned.
Confirmed No.10 and captain; creative hub, set-piece deliverer and primary penalty taker - value via free-kicks/penalty and late arrivals in the box.
Uzbekistan captain and all-time top scorer (44 in 92); joint Super Lig top scorer (22) - the only realistic Uzbek scoring threat, on the counter.

assists.note: Confirmed playmaker and set-piece deliverer; Colombia's chief chance-creator feeding Suarez and Diaz.
Confirmed left wing; 14 assists last season, his cutbacks and crosses a primary supply line to Suarez.
Confirmed overlapping right-back; high work-rate, repeated wide deliveries against a deep Uzbek block.

penalty.note: With Richard Rios out of the XI, James (captain, Colombia's established first-choice taker) is the clear penalty taker; Diaz the converting backup. Medium likelihood as Uzbekistan defend deep and concede fouls under sustained pressure.

lineups.home: Utkir Yusupov, Rustam Ashurmatov, Abdukodir Khusanov, Jakhongir Urozov, Farrukh Sayfiev, Otabek Shukurov, Akmal Mozgovoy, Sherzod Nasrullaev, Abbosbek Fayzullaev, Oston Urunov, Eldor Shomurodov
lineups.away: Camilo Vargas, Daniel Munoz, Davinson Sanchez, Jhon Lucumi, Johan Mojica, Jefferson Lerma, Gustavo Puerta, Jhon Arias, James Rodriguez, Luis Diaz, Luis Suarez

playerNotes.note: Confirmed striker; Sporting CP's 38-goal Primeira Liga golden boot, on his major-tournament debut he is the focal striker every move runs through.
Confirmed left wing in best form of his career off a Bundesliga title at Bayern (15 G/14 A); pace and dribbling test Uzbekistan's right side.
Confirmed No.10 and captain; the creative hub, set-piece and penalty taker - Colombia's tempo and final-third quality flow through him.
The change in the confirmed XI: partners Lerma in the double pivot ahead of the omitted Richard Rios; screens the back four and controls midfield at altitude.
Man City defender anchoring the (probable) Uzbek back three, but injury-curtailed minutes raise sharpness concerns against Diaz/Suarez.
Uzbekistan's talisman and record scorer; isolated as the lone counter outlet in a 3-4-2-1, dependent on Fayzullaev behind him.

win.reason: Czechia field both Schick and Hložek up top in a 3-5-2 with Krejčí an aerial set-piece weapon from the back three (he already headed home the opener vs South Korea), and the Czechs scored the most set-piece goals in UEFA qualifying (10). But South Africa under Broos defend in a disciplined deep block with elite keeper Williams behind it, and lost their openers only narrowly in shape, so a draw is genuinely live.

scorers.note: Leverkusen's form striker (16 Bundesliga goals, 9 in his last 7), Czech qualifying top-scorer and penalty taker; main threat but treated measured given SA's deep block and Williams behind it — not a banker.

Set-piece aerial weapon from the back three; already scored the headed opener vs South Korea and the Czechs convert heavily from corners/long throws — South Africa's biggest tracking problem at dead balls.
Leads the SA line in Foster's absence; 12 PSL goals for Sundowns this season at 0.76/90, the focal point for any counter-attacking break.
Partners Schick up top in the 3-5-2; mobile second striker who drops and runs the channels, a secondary finisher off Czech build-up and set-piece scraps.

assists.note: SA's most direct outlet — pace and width on the flank, draws fouls and delivers; also a free-kick/corner option, the likeliest creator on the break.
Right wing-back delivering crosses into a box stacked with Schick, Hložek and Krejčí; Czechia attempted the most crosses in qualifying (284).
Pace on the opposite flank; SA's counter-attack runs through Maseko and Appollis in transition feeding Rayners.

penalty.note: Schick is Czechia's designated penalty taker. For South Africa, with Mofokeng and Foster on the bench, Appollis is the on-pitch option (Mokoena has taken none in his career). Note Williams is an elite penalty-saver — first keeper to save four in an AFCON shootout, 2024 best-GK — which lowers the value of any Czech spot-kick.

lineups.home: 3-5-2: Kovář; Hranáč, Holeš, Krejčí; Coufal, Sadílek, Darida, Černý, Sojka; Hložek, Schick
lineups.away: 4-3-3: Williams; Mudau, Okon, Mbokazi, Modiba; Mokoena, Mbatha, Adams; Maseko, Rayners, Appollis

playerNotes.note: 30 and fully fit (no aging-decline case), top of his game with 16 Bundesliga goals; Czech penalty taker and main goal threat, but kept measured given SA's deep block.
Set-piece aerial threat from the back three; scored the opener vs South Korea and won the most defensive-third possessions of any UEFA qualifier — danger at both boxes.
Captain and elite shot-stopper; first keeper to save four penalties in an AFCON shootout (2024), ranked a top-10 world GK — anchors a disciplined deep block.
Deep-lying SA midfielder who strikes from distance and free kicks (scored a famous FK vs Morocco), but has never taken a penalty — set-piece delivery, not the pen man.
Starts up front with Foster benched; in-form Sundowns striker (12 league goals) tasked with leading the line and finishing rare chances on the break.
Dropped from the confirmed XI to the bench alongside Provod and Šulc; a major aerial set-piece game-changer Czechia can introduce, as SA can bring Mofokeng and Foster.

win.reason: Switzerland are unbeaten in competitive matches since late 2024 and conceded only twice in qualifying; Bosnia rely on a 40-year-old Dzeko and are nursing injury doubts (Tabakovic out, Kolasinac knock).

scorers.note: Leading Swiss scorer (24 intl goals) and penalty taker; converted from the spot vs Qatar in the opener.
Pacey wide threat cutting in; key chance-creator for Switzerland in the final third.
Direct left-sided winger who carries Switzerland's attacking output alongside Ndoye.
Bosnia's most mobile forward and Dzeko's foil; their likeliest source of a goal.

assists.note: Dictates Swiss tempo and set-piece delivery; the main creative hub.
Creates chances from left-back and takes set pieces for Switzerland.
Breakout PSV winger; Bosnia's chief creator and clutch performer in the play-offs.

penalty.note: Embolo took and scored Switzerland's penalty against Qatar; he is the established taker with Xhaka as senior backup.

lineups.home: Kobel; Zakaria, Akanji, Elvedi; Aebischer, Sow, Xhaka, Rodriguez; Ndoye, Embolo, Vargas
lineups.away: Vasilj; Kolasinac, Muharemovic, Katic, Dedic; Memic, Tahirovic, Basic, Bajraktarevic; Demirovic, Lukic

playerNotes.note: Leads the line and takes penalties; scored in the 1-1 opener vs Qatar.
Captain and metronome who controls tempo and dead balls.
Anchors a back three that conceded only twice across qualifying.
40-year-old talisman and record scorer (73 goals); rescued the play-off vs Wales but likely managed minutes.
Captain and left-back carrying a knock from the Canada draw; a fitness doubt to start.
Forward ruled out / not match fit due to an ankle injury, thinning Bosnia's attacking options.

win.reason: Confirmed Canada XI (Crepeau; Johnston, Cornelius, De Fougerolles, Laryea; Buchanan, Eustaquio, Kone, Ahmed; Larin, J.David) fields its full first-choice attack with Davies held in reserve, and at BC Place the co-hosts should dominate a Qatar 4-3-3 that mustered only 0.76 xG, 6 shots and 32% possession vs Switzerland. Opta sims Canada at 72.9%, books ~1.28-1.29. But Qatar's Madibo-Gaber-Laye screen plus the Khoukhi/Pedro Miguel block frustrated a Swiss side that ran up 3.24 xG and still only drew via a stoppage-time leveller, exactly the deep-block-out-xG'd-yet-survives pattern; Canada have never won a WC match in 7 tries and were wasteful vs Bosnia. Draw is live at +470, so DNB at ~1.55 is the honest price on a real ~55-60% clean Canada edge, not implied certainty.

scorers.note: Confirmed starter and Canada's penalty taker (scored twice from the spot vs Iceland in warm-up); shortest anytime price at +110. NOT a banker - poor recent form, 1 goal since early Feb at Juventus, and blazed a chance over vs Bosnia.
Now starts after his super-sub volley equaliser vs Bosnia; penalty backup behind David. Aging marquee striker - explicitly NOT a banker, streaky finisher reliant on service.
Confirmed RW, rediscovered form at Villarreal; cuts inside onto his left, secondary goal route if Qatar sit deep.

Qatar's confirmed talisman, pen + free-kick + corner taker; the only realistic away scorer on the counter. Qatar created almost nothing vs Switzerland (0.76 xG), so this is value-against-the-grain, not a likely event.

assists.note: Confirmed start over Davies/Millar - Vancouver Whitecaps academy product getting a homecoming start; ball-carrying creator from the left, a live assist source vs a deep block.
Whips crosses for David/Larin from the right; primary supply line against a low Qatar block.
Record 10 assists at 2019 Asian Cup; takes Qatar's corners/free-kicks, so any Qatar goal (incl. a Khoukhi/Pedro Miguel set-piece header) likely runs through him.

penalty.backup: Cyle Larin (Canada); Akram Afif for Qatar
penalty.note: Canada dominate the ball and David is the squad's most-fouled-in-box player + designated taker, so a Canada spot-kick is the most probable; if Qatar win one it's Afif. Deep-block congestion keeps this only moderate, not high.

lineups.home: Crepeau; Johnston, Cornelius, de Fougerolles, Laryea; Buchanan, Eustaquio, Kone, Ali Ahmed; J. David, Larin
lineups.away: Abunada; Al Oui, Pedro Miguel, Khoukhi, Homam El Amin; Madibo, Gaber, Issa Laye; Edmilson Junior, Abdurisag, Afif

playerNotes.note: FIT but named only on the bench - Marsch trusts the settled Buchanan/Ahmed wide pairing and is easing Davies back, so Canadas first-half ceiling is slightly lower than full strength; a strong 60-70min game-changer if Qatars block holds, which raises late-Canada-goal value.
Starts over Dayne St. Clair (Marsch called it the hardest decision of his career) - reliable but untested at this level, so any Crepeau error is a live tail risk on Qatars rare counters.
The pen taker and shortest anytime scorer (+110) but arrives in genuinely poor form (1 goal since early February) and missed a presentable chance vs Bosnia - do not treat him as a lock.
Earned this start off his super-sub equaliser vs Bosnia; an aging, service-dependent finisher - a sensible scorer flyer, never a banker.
Captain/CB who scored the stoppage-time leveller vs Switzerland - with Pedro Miguel he makes Qatar a genuine set-piece header threat off Afif deliveries, the main route to an away goal.

Top scorer in AFC qualifying, starts on the bench behind Abdurisag - a dangerous 60min+ introduction if Qatar chase, so a Qatar clean sheet AND a late Qatar goal both carry more weight than the lopsided xG suggests.

win.reason: Mexico are home in Guadalajara with a settled 4-3-3 and Edson Alvarez restoring midfield steel alongside Lira and Romo, plus Alvarado, Gutierrez and Quiñones giving width and creation around Jimenez. South Korea defend in a compact block off Kim Min-jae and counter through Son and Lee Kang-in, which makes this a genuine draw risk rather than a formality — Mexico are ~55% favourites, so the win is far from a certainty.

scorers.note: Leads the line and scored vs South Africa, but at 34 he is a finisher of chances, not a banker against a deep Korean block marshalled by Kim Min-jae.
Wide runner who gets in behind the Korean full-backs (Seol Young-woo, Kim Moon-hwan); a real cut-inside threat from the right channel.
Korea's primary goal threat and penalty taker, dangerous on the counter, but at 33 leading the line against Vasquez/Alvarez he is not a banker.
On the bench but Mexico's clearest impact sub — a genuine penalty-box striker who could be sent on to chase a goal against a tiring low block.

assists.note: Confirmed starter and a chief creator from the right/half-spaces feeding Jimenez and the runners.
Starting creator with line-breaking passing; key supply line into the box for Mexico.
Korea's chief creator and set-piece taker; his vision and delivery is the main route to feed Son on the break and from dead balls.

penalty.note: For Korea, Son is the established penalty taker with Lee Kang-in on free-kicks/backup. For Mexico, Raul Jimenez is the likely spot-kick taker, with Alvarado as an alternative; medium likelihood given Mexico's pressure into a packed box.

lineups.home: Rangel; Sanchez, Vasquez, Alvarez, Gallardo; Lira, Romo, Gutierrez; Alvarado, Quiñones, Jimenez
lineups.away: Kim Seung-gyu; Seol Young-woo, Kim Min-jae, Lee Han-beom, Kim Moon-hwan; Lee Gi-hyuk, Hwang In-beom, Paik Seung-ho; Lee Kang-in, Lee Jae-sung, Son Heung-min

playerNotes.note: Steps in for suspended Cesar Montes (red vs South Africa); his ball-winning and aerial presence shore up the back line and let Lira/Romo push.
Comes in for Alvaro Fidalgo to add legs and progression in the engine room of the 4-3-3.
Lone striker who scored in the 2-0 opener; focal point but reliant on service against a deep block.
Captain and talisman leading the line with Hwang Hee-chan benched; Korea's main outlet on the counter and penalty/free-kick threat.
Scored and assisted in the 2-1 comeback over Czechia; the deep-lying metronome who springs Korean counters to Son and Lee Kang-in.
Bayern anchor of the Korean back line; his organisation and aerial dominance are the wall Mexico must break to avoid a draw.

win.reason: USA are home in Seattle, hammered Paraguay 4-1 with a Balogun brace and have a deeper, more talented squad; Australia upset Turkey 2-0 but will sit deep and counter, so a USA win is most likely though the Socceroos' organisation keeps it from being a rout.

scorers.note: Scored twice in the 4-1 win over Paraguay; leads the USA line and is the most likely scorer.
Talisman and main creator; passed fit after a knock vs Paraguay, threat from the left and on set pieces.
Leads Australia's line; recovered from illness, dangerous on the counter against a high USA line.
Scored vs Turkey; pace and directness from the left make him Australia's chief transition threat.

assists.note: Primary creator and set-piece deliverer for the USA; feeds Balogun and the runners.
Attacking outlet down the right/front three; overlaps and crosses into the box.
Box-arriving midfielder who links play and supplies the final ball into the area.

penalty.note: Australia defend deep and compact so spot-kicks are not especially likely; Pulisic is USA's designated taker with Balogun as backup.

lineups.home: Freese; Freeman, Richards, Ream, Robinson; Adams, Tillman; Dest, McKennie, Pulisic; Balogun
lineups.away: Beach; Burgess, Circati, Souttar; Bos, O'Neill, Metcalfe, Italiano; Irankunda, Okon-Engstler; Toure

playerNotes.note: Two goals vs Paraguay; in form and leading the line as USA's primary scorer.
Took a knock vs Paraguay (off at half-time) but Pochettino confirmed he is fit to start; key creator.
Midfield anchor and captain; sets the press and shields the back line against Australia's counters.
Recovered from illness around the opener; fit and projected to lead the Socceroos line.
Scored in the 2-0 upset of Turkey; pace and directness make him Australia's main attacking threat.
Scored vs Turkey; plays off Toure in the 3-4-2-1 and provides midfield goal threat.

win.reason: Morocco's depth and quality (Hakimi, Brahim Diaz, Ounahi, Saibari) outclass Scotland, who edged Haiti 1-0; Morocco held Brazil 1-1 and need a win to control the group, while Scotland may sit deeper at home.

scorers.note: Scored vs Brazil and leads Morocco's line; their most reliable goal threat right now.
Real Madrid creator drifting inside; carries Morocco's attacking spark from the half-spaces.
Scored the winner vs Haiti arriving late into the box; Scotland's top in-form midfield runner.
Leads Scotland's line and links play; main central striker threat on the counter.

assists.note: Morocco captain and overlapping right-back; their primary wide chance-creator.
Scotland captain delivers from the left flank and set pieces.
Morocco's creative outlet between the lines feeding Saibari.

penalty.note: Hakimi is Morocco's captain and regular penalty option; an aggressive press in a tight game raises spot-kick chance.

lineups.home: Gunn; Hickey, Hendry, Hanley, Robertson; Gannon-Doak, Ferguson, McTominay, McGinn; Adams, Shankland
lineups.away: Bono; Hakimi, Diop, Riad, Mazraoui; El Aynaoui, Bouaddi; Diaz, Ounahi, El Khannouss; Saibari

playerNotes.note: Napoli midfielder drives Scotland from midfield; hit post and headed over early vs Haiti.
Scored the winner vs Haiti; Scotland's energy and late-box arrival.
Calf doubt for this match; defensive depth concern for Clarke.
Captain recovered from thigh issue; world-class attacking right-back and main threat.
Scored vs Brazil; leads the line as Morocco's sharpest finisher.
Real Madrid star pulling strings in Morocco's attacking midfield.

win.reason: Brazil are a class above and drew their opener 1-0 over Morocco via a Vinicius strike, while Haiti lost 1-0 to Scotland and will sit deep in a 4-2-3-1 for damage limitation; the talent gap (Vinicius, Raphinha, Cunha vs a defence missing pace) makes a comfortable Brazil win the overwhelming outcome.

scorers.note: Scored Brazil's winner vs Morocco; primary attacking outlet on the left against a deep, slow Haiti back line.
Leads the line ahead of Igor Thiago; expected to start centrally and feast on chances vs a damage-limitation defence.
Brazil's most consistently threatening player vs Morocco and the team's penalty/set-piece taker; due a goal.
Advanced midfield runner arriving in the box; chips in against deep blocks.

assists.note: Brazil's chief creator and dead-ball deliverer from the right; high crossing/cutback volume vs a packed box.
Constant left-side overloads and cutbacks; creates as much as he scores.
Provided the assist vs Morocco; deep-lying progressor threading passes through the block.

penalty.note: Haiti will defend deep and concede fouls/handballs inside their box under sustained Brazil pressure, raising spot-kick odds; Raphinha is the designated taker.

lineups.home: Alisson; Danilo, Marquinhos, Gabriel Magalhaes, Douglas Santos; Casemiro, Bruno Guimaraes; Raphinha, Lucas Paqueta, Vinicius Junior; Matheus Cunha
lineups.away: Johnny Placide; Arcus, Delcroix, Ricardo Ade, Experience; Bellegarde, Jean; Deedson, Casimir, Providence; Wilson Isidor

playerNotes.note: Scored the 1-0 winner vs Morocco; fit and central to Brazil's chances.
Remains doubtful/sidelined — only just returned to light training and trains mostly alone; unlikely to start.
Most consistently threatening vs Morocco; Brazil's penalty and set-piece taker.
Expected to start up front ahead of Igor Thiago after Ancelotti's rotation.
Captain and most experienced player; will face heavy shot volume behind a deep block.
Sunderland striker who switched from France to Haiti; lone forward and main outlet on the counter.

win.reason: Türkiye dominated their opener with 30 shots but lost 2-0 to Australia, while Paraguay were exposed defensively in a 4-1 loss to the USA; Montella's side has superior attacking depth (Akturkoglu, Guler, Yildiz) and home-quarter momentum in a must-win game.

scorers.note: Turkiye's most lethal forward (15 international goals); leads the line in a must-win against a leaky Paraguay back line.
Captain and primary penalty/set-piece taker (22 international goals); danger from spot-kicks and free-kicks.
Creative spark for Turkiye who can also finish; central to Montella's chance creation.
Paraguay's lead striker, scored four goals in qualifying; main outlet if La Albirroja counter.

assists.note: Created five chances against Australia from left-back; Turkiye's most prolific chance creator in the opener.
Primary playmaker linking midfield to attack for Turkiye.
Paraguay's key creative force in the front four.

penalty.note: Turkiye attack heavily and Paraguay's defence was breached four times by the USA; Calhanoglu is the established spot-kick taker (three international penalties scored, plus one in qualifying vs Bulgaria).

lineups.home: Cakir; Celik, Demiral, Bardakci, Kadioglu; Calhanoglu, Yuksek; Guler, Kokcu, Yilmaz; Akturkoglu
lineups.away: Gill; Caceres, G. Gomez, Alderete, Maidana; Bobadilla, Cubas; D. Gomez, Enciso, Almiron; Sanabria

playerNotes.note: Calf problem; likely only a bench option after struggling in the opener, so Yilmaz/Akturkoglu start.
Created five chances vs Australia; Turkiye's attacking outlet from left-back.
Captain, deep-lying playmaker and penalty/free-kick taker (105 caps, 22 goals).
Captain and centre-back anchoring a defence that conceded four to the USA.
Veteran winger (77 caps) providing experience and width in the front four.
Lead striker; four qualifying goals, Paraguay's main scoring threat on the counter.
